pattern: Fixing the middle index turns the triplet count into a product of how many doubles sit to its left and to its right, accumulated with running frequency maps.
hints: The middle element nums[j] decides everything: both the left and the right element must equal twice nums[j]. So fix j and count matches on each side.
Sweep left to right keeping a frequency map of values already seen (candidates for i) and a frequency map of values still ahead (candidates for k).
At each j add left_count[2*nums[j]] * right_count[2*nums[j]] to the answer modulo 1e9+7, then move nums[j] from the right map into the left map.

edge_inputs.description: single triplet with the double on both ends
doubles exist but not on both sides of a middle
all the same nonzero value forms no triplet
all zeros, every ordered triple qualifies

stress.description: random small values so doubling relationships occur often
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(3000, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

solutions.python:
from collections import Counter

class Solution:
    def specialTriplets(self, nums):
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        left = Counter()
        right = Counter(nums)
        ans = 0
        for v in nums:
            right[v] -= 1
            t = v * 2
            ans = (ans + left[t] * right[t]) % MOD
            left[v] += 1
        return ans
